title: IIIF manifest alignment for image-heavy collections
output: IIIF manifest alignment module (TypeScript) committed via PR, with valid Presentation API v3 output.
acceptanceCriteria: The cleared-item contract for image items includes a IIIF manifest reference where LoC provides one.
A IIIF manifest generation or alignment layer is implemented for collections without a native IIIF manifest.
The IIIF manifest includes required courtesy attribution and rights metadata.
Integration tests confirm the manifests are valid IIIF Presentation API v3.
pnpm build and pnpm test and pnpm lint pass.
context: IIIF (International Image Interoperability Framework) is the standard protocol for sharing images across digital-library platforms. Aligning cleared-item records with IIIF manifests improves commons interoperability so Wikimedia Commons, Internet Archive, and library platforms can ingest them directly. Unscheduled backlog; improves downstream ingestion for image-heavy LoC collections.
objective: Add IIIF manifest alignment to the cleared-item fan-out contract for image-heavy collections, enabling downstream commons and library platforms to ingest cleared items via standard IIIF viewers and aggregators.
